---
title: "Alignment Status"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook for trialing the data cleaning/processing/analysis for the project "Status and Conversational Convergence"

David Heath

The goal of this notebook is to extract transcript data from the CANDOR dataset and convert those transcripts into numerical indexes for Recurrence Quantification Analysis using the crqa package.

#Set Up
```{r}
library(writexl)
library(readxl)
library(crqa)

#clear workspace

rm(list=ls())

#Set Working Directory

#setwd(D:/CANDOR)
setwd("C:/Users/daheath/OneDrive - The University of Memphis/Heath/Alignment Status/CANDOR/cliffhanger transcripts")


```



#Data Processing

Here we are going through the CANDOR dataset files (now stored on Lab Team drive (see PATH above)), reading each transcript (currently using the cliffhanger version), separating the transcript into individual words, and then indexing those words. This reads a \*.csv file and outputs a \*.txt file containing the transcript as raw text and a \*.csv file containing the word-level transcript with index numbers. This new \*.csv file can be used by the crqa package. 

Might be able to make a simpler version that can go from csv to csv w/o intermediary txt file?

##Extract text

```{r}
# Specify the directory containing the CSV files

#this is where the dataset lives

csv.directory <- getwd()

# List all CSV files in the directory
csv.files <- list.files(path = csv.directory,
                        pattern = "transcript_cliffhanger.csv",
                        full.names = TRUE,
                        recursive = TRUE,
                        include.dirs = TRUE)

# Initialize an empty list to store data frames
data.list <- list()

# Loop through each CSV file and read it into a data frame
for (file in csv_files) {
  data <- read.csv(file)
  data.list[[file]] <- data
  save.path <- gsub("transcript_cliffhanger.csv", "",file)
  output.file <- file.path(save.path,"/transcript_cliffhanger.txt")
  string.data <- data$utterance
  
  # Write the string data to a text file, one entry per line
  writeLines(as.character(string.data), con = output.file)
  }
```

##Asign Indexes & Write New File

```{r}
# convert to numerical indexes

#This uses the SAME directory as chuck above.
#If running only this chunk, delete # below

#this is where the dataset lives
#csv_directory <- "D:/CANDOR"

#this is where our test set lives
#csv_directory <- "C:/Users/daheath/OneDrive - The University of Memphis/Heath/CANDOR test crqa"

# List all CSV files in the directory
txt.files <- list.files(path = csv.directory,
                        pattern = "transcript_cliffhanger.txt",
                        full.names = TRUE,
                        recursive = TRUE,
                        include.dirs = TRUE)

# Initialize an empty list to store data frames
txt.list <- list()

# Loop through each CSV file and read it into a data frame
for (file in txt.files) {
  
  #read text file
  txt.data <- readLines(file) 
  txt.list[[file]] <- txt.data
  
  #combining into single string
  txt.combined <- paste(txt.data, collapse = " ") 
  
  #split text into words
  words<- unlist(strsplit(txt.combined, "\\s+")) 
  
  #remove punct, convert to lowercase
  clean.words <- tolower(gsub("[[:punct:]]", "", words)) 
  
  # Create a named vector where each unique word gets a unique index
  unique.words <- unique(clean.words)
  word.indices <- setNames(seq_along(unique.words), unique.words)
  
  # Map the original words to their respective indices
  indexed.vector <- word.indices[clean_words]
  
  # get file path
  txt.save.path <- gsub("transcript_cliffhanger.txt", "",file)
  
  # save the unique word-to-index mapping to a file
  txt.output.file <- file.path(txt.save.path, "transcript_cliffhanger_indexed.csv")
  write.csv(data.frame(Word = words, Index = indexed_vector),
            file = txt.output.file, row.names = FALSE)
  #writeLines(as.character(indexed_vector), con = txt_output_file)
  
}

```

#Recurrence Quantification Analysis

```{r}
#this is a test crqa script to
#run an autoreccurance on a CANDOR 
#transcript, using the cliffhanger
#version
#code adapted from Alex Paxton's crqa tutorial

#This uses the SAME directory as chuck above.
#If running only this chunk, delete # below

#this is where the dataset lives
#csv_directory <- "D:/CANDOR"

#this is where our test set lives
#csv_directory <- "C:/Users/daheath/OneDrive - The University of Memphis/Heath/CANDOR test crqa"

#### ind = indexed #### 

# List all CSV files in the directory
ind.files <- list.files(path = csv.directory,
                        pattern = "transcript_cliffhanger_indexed.csv",
                        full.names = TRUE,
                        recursive = TRUE,
                        include.dirs = TRUE)

# Initialize an empty list to store data frames
ind.list <- list()

# Create df for storing RQA outputs  
rqa.metrics <- data.frame(matrix(nrow=1,ncol=10), c("CONVO_ID",
  "RR",
  "DET",
  "NRLINE",
  "maxL",
  "L",
  "ENTR",
  "rENTR",
  "LAM",
  "TT"))

metric.names<-`c("CONVO_ID",
  "RR",
  "DET",
  "NRLINE",
  "maxL",
  "L",
  "ENTR",
  "rENTR",
  "LAM",
  "TT"))

colnames(rqa_metrics) <- metric.names 

# Loop through each CSV file and read it into a data frame
for (file in ind.files) {
  
  #get CONVO ID 
  convoID.left.extract <- gsub("C:/Users/daheath/OneDrive - The University of Memphis/","",file)
  convoID <- gsub("/CANDOR test crqa/transcript_cliffhanger_indexed.csv", "", convoID.left.extract)

  # set the Theiler window parameter for RQA (should be 1 to ignore LOI in RQA)
  rec_tw_quantification = 1

  # set radius to be very small for categorical matches
  rec_categorical_radius = .0001

  ######## 3b. Run recurrence quantification analysis ########

  # run rqa over informative text
  recurrence_analysis_transcript = crqa(ts1=data[2],
                                       ts2=data[2],
                                       delay=0,
                                       embed=2,
                                       rescale=0,
                                       radius=rec_categorical_radius,
                                       normalize=0,
                                       mindiagline=2,
                                       minvertline=2,
                                       tw=rec_tw_quantification)

  ######## 3c. Create the recurrence plot ########

  # Note: In order to get the line of identity to appear in these plots,
  #       you must run another `crqa()` function call with a Theiler window
  #       (`tw`) of 0 in order to preserve the line of identity in the plot.

  # set the Theiler window parameter for RP (should be 0 to keep LOI in RP)
  rec_tw_plot = 0

  # run rqa over informative with a Theiler window of 0 for plotting
  recurrence_analysis_plot_transcript = crqa(ts1=data[2],
                                            ts2=data[2],
                                            delay=0,
                                            embed=2,
                                            rescale=0,
                                            radius=rec_categorical_radius,
                                            normalize=0,
                                            mindiagline=2,
                                            minvertline=2,
                                            tw=rec_tw_plot)


  # use crqa package's native plotting function
  par = list(unit = 2, 
           labelx = "Letter", 
           labely = "Letter", 
           cols = "purple", 
           pcex = 1)
  plot_rp(recurrence_analysis_plot_transcript$RP, par)

# take a look at the quantification metrics for informative text
#recurrence_analysis_transcript$RR # rate of recurrence
#recurrence_analysis_transcript$DET # % determinism
#recurrence_analysis_transcript$NRLINE # total number of lines on the plot
#recurrence_analysis_transcript$maxL # maximum line length on plot
#recurrence_analysis_transcript$L # average line length on plot
#recurrence_analysis_transcript$ENTR # entropy
#recurrence_analysis_transcript$rENTR # normalized entropy
#recurrence_analysis_transcript$LAM # laminarity
recurrence_analysis_transcript$TT # trapping time

## Next we need to write the values ^ into a data.frame. Ideally WITH the conversation ID. 

rqa_metrics_hold <- cbind(
  convoID,
  recurrence_analysis_transcript$RR, # rate of recurrence
  recurrence_analysis_transcript$DET, # % determinism
  recurrence_analysis_transcript$NRLINE, # total number of lines on the plot
  recurrence_analysis_transcript$maxL, # maximum line length on plot
  recurrence_analysis_transcript$L, # average line length on plot
  recurrence_analysis_transcript$ENTR, # entropy
  recurrence_analysis_transcript$rENTR, # normalized entropy
  recurrence_analysis_transcript$LAM, # laminarity
  recurrence_analysis_transcript$TT) # trapping time

  for(i in (ind_files)){
    rqa_metrics <- rbind(rqa_metrics, rqa_metrics_hold)}
}
```

Append not working yet - I think I need to add a cbind before rqa_metrics_hold
